content.full_text:
Memorandum

To: File

Date: Jan. 3, 1978

From: FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION  
Ted Prim

Subject: Banker's Acceptance Notes, Line of Credit and Loans against Margin

At the opinion request meeting of December 16, 1978, the staff concluded that banker's acceptance notes are not a reportable investment. The rationale for this decision is that, based on the information provided to us, it was our understanding that banker's acceptance notes were essentially analogous to cashier's checks with a time limit.

A line of credit is reportable. The value of the line of credit is the maximum amount which the official is permitted to borrow at any one time during the period.

Loans borrowed against a margin account maintained with a stock brokerage firm are reportable on the basis of the maximum amount which is borrowed at any one time during the period covered by the statement.

TP:nrw
